# Agent SDLC

> A full-SDLC pipeline for AI coding agents — idea to a reviewed pull request — packaged as a Claude Code + Cursor plugin, with a zero-dependency enforcement checker (sdlc-check) and three standalone documentation skills.

The skills in `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` are the authoritative method; `docs/` is the user- and
contributor-facing guide; `docs/specs/` is the repo's own pipeline output (it builds itself through the
pipeline it implements).

## Docs

- [README](README.md): the front door — what it is, quickstart, install, layout.
- [Quickstart](docs/quickstart.md): install + first run in two commands.
- [Example run](docs/example-run.md): a five-minute worked example, idea to reviewed PR.
- [Install](docs/install.md): Claude Code, Cursor, other agents, updates, requirements.
- [The pipeline](docs/usage/pipeline.md): the stage chain, the traceability spine, the artifact model.
- [Start anywhere](docs/usage/start-anywhere.md): enter at any stage, from any source.
- [The light tier](docs/usage/light-tier.md): the compressed pass for small changes.
- [sdlc-check](docs/usage/sdlc-check.md): the enforcement checker — invocation, rules, exit codes.
- [Linear sync](docs/usage/linear-sync.md): the optional Linear mirror.
- [Documentation skills](docs/usage/documentation-skills.md): writing-readmes / writing-repo-docs / writing-technical-docs.
- [Development](docs/development.md): clone, tests, skill authoring, releases.
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): the repo's shape in one page.
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): branch/merge flow, commit convention, what review expects.

## Optional

- [Project overview (living spec)](docs/specs/overview.md): the pipeline applied to this repo itself.
- [Skill: getting-started](skills/getting-started/SKILL.md): the router + shared operating rules.
